other_findings.finding: Reynolds et al. (2021) Fig. 3C legend refers to 'enrichment of gene signatures for murine splenic Xcr1+DC (DC1)', confirming DC1 corresponds to cDC1/XCR1+ DCs
DC1 cells are observed in embryonic skin as early as 7 PCW, prior to bone marrow hematopoiesis
Human cDC1 (DC1) are CLEC9A+XCR1+CD141+ and are specialised for cross-presentation of antigens to CD8+ T cells via MHC class I
In the PAGA analysis, DC1 and DC2 form one branch of the three mononuclear phagocyte differentiation trajectories (alongside LC and mo-DC branches)

evidence_quotes.quote: We observe Dendritic Cells 1 and 2 (DC1, DC2) and Langerhans cells (LCs) in embryonic skin as early as 7 PCW, prior to bone marrow hematopoiesis
evidence_quotes.source_file: Reynolds et al. (2021) PDF full text

evidence_quotes.quote: CLEC9A or DNGR1 is a C-type lectin receptor that mediates endocytosis, but not phagocytosis, with low pH endosomes promoting the drift toward cross-presentation. Importantly, CLEC9A binding of antigens induces antigen presentation on both MHC I (cross-presentation) and MHC II. It is highly and specifically expressed on CD11c+ CD141+ XCR1+ cDCs
evidence_quotes.source_file: Gornati et al. (2018) Dendritic Cells in the Cross Hair for the Generation of Tailored Vaccines. PMC6030256
